article.title: Docker - Kubernetes
article.content:
Hi folks , 


I m learning Docker and Kubernetes by my own using Youtube and Documentation but feeling stucks like what to do , and what to avoid ?

If anyone has a great background in these please suggest . 

I m thinking to start it again from scratch . 

Thanks in advance.

post.content: Kodekloud is a great place to start

Build a Rest Service 
deploy on aws using a docker file 
publish an image to ECR use this image for deployment 
Deploy an opensource app on kubernetes using Helm for deployment 
By the end of this exercise you would have touched upon the topics in a more hands on way
no don\'t quit.... You can opt any courses or can follow Harkirat bhaiya\'s YT channel or ask your doubts to GPT..... docker and k8s docs is not beginner friendly....

Start with Docker first, learn containers, images, Dockerfiles, and Docker Compose. Once you\'re comfortable, move to Kubernetes: focus on Pods, Deployments, Services, and ConfigMaps. Use official documentation and small hands-on projects to practice. Don\u2019t try to master everything at once, build a strong foundation first, then explore automation and CI/CD later.
